name: Leucrota
primary_sources: On the Characteristics of Animals by Claudius Aelianus

visual_attributes.morphology: hybrid creature with features of multiple animals
distinctive_features: mouth opening right back to the ears
ridges of bone in place of rows of teeth
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: about the size of an ass
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: body parts of various animals blended together

domain_specific_visual.MYTHICAL_HYBRID: combination of dog, wolf, lion, stag, and badger features

functional_attributes.primary_function: mimic human voices to lure prey

domain_specific_functional.MYTHICAL_ROLE: deadly enemy of men and dogs

relationships.description: Believed to be a hybrid between a hyena and a lion
Believed to be a hybrid between a hyena and a lion

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: mythically located in India and Aethiopia
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents fear and deception, linked to oracular powers

contrastive_constraints: NOT a simple wolf or dog
NOT a purely fictional creature without historical context
